task_completion.description: Log into the Oscar EMR system using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Oscar EMR login screen with 'oscardoc' and '1117' entered, followed by the main Oscar EMR dashboard being visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the eForm administration or upload section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the Oscar EMR administration page specifically for managing, adding, or uploading eForms.

task_completion.description: Select the correct source file for the eForm template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file upload dialog or file input field showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/pain_assessment.html' selected for upload.

task_completion.description: Enter the required name for the eForm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Rapid Pain Assessment' is typed into the eForm name or title input field.

task_completion.description: Ensure the form is successfully registered and active in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The eForm library or list of uploaded forms shows 'Rapid Pain Assessment' present, and its status is indicated as active, saved, or available for use.

integrity.description: The agent used the Oscar EMR web interface to upload the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser navigating Oscar EMR, rather than using terminal commands (like curl or API scripts) to upload the file.

integrity.description: The agent uploaded the provided HTML file rather than manually recreating it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file picker or upload input is used to select the existing 'pain_assessment.html' file, rather than the agent copy-pasting code into a text area.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to register the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL/PostgreSQL prompts or SQL INSERT commands related to eForms.
